name: worlds-backup
description: How to snapshot a singleplayer world before risky changes and roll it back safely. Use when the user wants to back up or restore a save ("备份存档","回档","恢复存档","world backup","restore world").
body:
# Worlds backup & restore

Goal: never let a risky world edit become permanent.

## Calls
- `instance(action=worlds_backup_create, instance, world)` — timestamped snapshot; auto-prunes
  beyond retention. The ONLY backup mechanism now.
- `instance(action=worlds_backup_list, instance, world)` — snapshots newest-first, with `backupId`.
- `instance(action=worlds_backup_restore, instance, world, backupId)` — DESTRUCTIVE, red-confirm,
  auto-backs-up current state first.

## Rules
- Before ANY restore, delete, or NBT edit on a world: `worlds_backup_create` first. Always.
- NEVER restore while the game has that world open — ask the user to quit first.
- Don't guess the backupId: `worlds_backup_list`, let the user pick via `ask`, then restore with
  the chosen id.
- No world named: `worlds_list` first to confirm which one, then back it up; report the retention
  count.
